nodes.speaker: Lightningclaw
nodes.text: You're not out of breath. Good. Start. No - not there, the post on the left, the one Thunderstrike hasn't broken yet. I am Lightningclaw, trainer of everything StormClan warriors learn after they've finished being polite. Did you come to run or to talk?

options.text: To run. What are you teaching?

nodes.speaker: Lightningclaw
nodes.text: Two techniques, both practical. Speed Burst - short, explosive acceleration that takes an enemy out of kicking range before their back paws have answered the question. Chain Lightning - for when the enemy brings friends. You strike one, the air takes the rest. Thunderstrike teaches the roar and dash; I teach the ugly, useful middle parts.

nodes.speaker: Lightningclaw
nodes.text: Speed Burst. Always. If you cannot disengage, no other trick matters because you will die before casting it. A warrior who can leave a fight on their own terms is a warrior I can teach anything else. A warrior who cannot is a lesson the enemy finishes for me.

options.text: Speed Burst first, then. Show me.

nodes.speaker: Lightningclaw
nodes.text: Low stance. Rear paws wide, front paws light. The burst is not in your legs - it is in your ribs. Breathe in sharp, then throw your entire chest forward and let your paws catch up. Yes. Do it again. Faster. Faster. Good. You'll ache tomorrow; that's how you know you did it correctly.

nodes.speaker: Lightningclaw
nodes.text: You've got the basic shape. It will grow faster the more you bleed for it. Come back when you want Chain Lightning - but earn Speed Burst first in an actual fight, or Chain Lightning won't save you.

nodes.speaker: Lightningclaw
nodes.text: Have you used Speed Burst in a real fight yet? ... I see. Come back when you have. Chain Lightning is a second-fight technique. The first fight teaches you what a real enemy looks like; then we can talk about hitting three of them at once.

nodes.speaker: Lightningclaw
nodes.text: A training accident. I was eight seasons younger and thought I could match a full-grown StormClan warrior at Chain Lightning without having mastered the breath for it. The backlash traveled through my shoulder and stayed. I keep the wrap on to remind students that the sky does not forgive laziness. Are you paying attention?

options.text: Yes. What should I learn first?

nodes.speaker: Lightningclaw
nodes.text: Good. Training is conversation the body can have with itself when you are too tired to think. Every drill I give you today is a sentence your paws will remember when your mind has quit. That is the only reason training works. Now - what are you here to learn?

nodes.speaker: Lightningclaw
nodes.text: Watch then. But stand back from the strike posts - I have hit bystanders I liked more than you, and they still limp. When you're ready to do more than watch, the ground will still be here.
